## Search Results

Date:
1821
Description:
A written receipt for six dollars addressed to Mrs. Drayton in 1821.

[![Receipt for Mrs. Drayton, 1821](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/271765/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/271765)

Date:
1837
Description:
A diploma for James S. Drayton certifying that he passed his courses in Chemistry, Natural Philosophy and Natural History.

[![Diploma for James. S. Drayton, 1837](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/271770/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/271770)

Date:
1839
Description:
An official letter stating that James S. Drayton was appointed to Third Corporal in the Charleston Ancient Artillery.

[![Appointment to Third Corporal for James Drayton, 1839](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/271771/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/271771)

Date:
1855
Description:
A signed letter stating that Mr. James Drayton is an honorary member of the Marion Artillery.

[![James Drayton Military Certification, 1855](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/271768/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/271768)

Date:
1856
Description:
A conveyance of property to James Drayton from his brothers Thomas and John Drayton following the death of their mother Mary. Included in the conveyance are the following "negroes": Beck and her infant, Dick, Tyrah, Kate, Isaac, Charlie, Isaac the house servant, Tom, Phoeby, Joe, Nelly and her infant, Dianna, William, Lizzy, Miley, Mary, Jerry, Hannah, Bess, and Moses.

[![Conveyance of Property to James Drayton, 1856](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/271769/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/271769)

Date:
1862
Description:
A tax receipt for J. Drayton listing his "negroes" and their value.

[![Receipt for County and State Tax for J. Drayton, 1862](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/271767/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/271767)

Date:
1863-05-22
Description:
Note from Stohenthal accompanying an enclosure of $12.500 (in Confederate Depository Certificate), for Drayton's 108 cotton bales. Enclosure not found.

[![S.S. Stohenthal to Thomas Drayton, May 22, 1863](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23851/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25708)

Date:
1863
Description:
Military charge against Victor "Neely" from the Palmetto Battalion of Light Artillery for obtaining leave from the military for one day, but not returning until a few days later.

[![Military Charge Against Victory Neely, 1863](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/271775/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/271775)

Date:
1863
Description:
A document listing medicinal items needed for the hands working at "Ben's Ferry."

[![Drayton Family Requisition for Medicine for Hands at Work, 1863](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/271780/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/271780)

Date:
1863
Description:
An official letter stating that James Drayton and "lady" were granted approval to visit St. Andrew's Parish.

[![Military Approval for Leave for James Drayton, 1863](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/271772/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/271772)

Date:
1864
Description:
A letter to James Drayton discussing twenty dollars sent to him.

[![Money for James Drayton, 1864](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/271773/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/271773)

Date:
1863-08-03
Description:
Geddings requests medical officers write to his office with details on their appointments and contracts. Miscellaneous ration information on back.

[![Circular to 1st Sub-division, 1st Military District](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23825/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25586)

Date:
1834
Description:
Handwritten copy by James S. Drayton

[![Poetry: Hornby Melodies No. 7](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23845/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25682)

Date:
1850-1865
Description:
Brief record of account between Doctor H.L. Toomer and James Drayton. All billed items are food. Miscellaneous calculations on back.

[![Account summary](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23822/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25572)

Description:
An account with J. S. Drayton listing items and prices.

[![Financial Account with J. S. Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/271774/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/271774)

Date:
1866-02-09
Description:
Acknowledging the receipt of $220 dollars from John Drayton in exchange for six horses. Miscellaneous names listed on back - probably freedmen

[![Receipt and list of names](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23850/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25704)

Date:
1850-1865
Description:
Slaves at Drayton Hall and slaves working in town ("Town List"). Slaves are listed along with clothing allocation (in yards). Divided into "Men, Women & Children" categories. Plantation slaves also divided by field and "House servants".

[![Slave and clothing inventories](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23854/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25722)

Date:
1860
Description:
Cloth and blanket list for slaves. Divided by sex and location (Drayton Hall or country; or "town")

[![Slave and clothing inventory](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23855/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25726)

Date:
1868-12-15
Description:
Drayton's attorneys reveal a new lease for Drayton Hall has been agreed with a mining company from the North. They hope to remove the current lessee, F.H. Trenholm, as soon as possible. Fees are due for ejecting Moulton Emery and John Prentice (the first lesses).

[![Clifford and Mattthews to John Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23827/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25594)

Date:
1868-12-04
Description:
Clifford and Matthews discuss the lease of Drayton Hall to F.H. Trenholm, his poor performance, and their hopes of replacing him as a lessee

[![Clifford and Matthews to John Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23826/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25590)

Description:
This document is a tax return for J.H.M. and John Drayton in St. Andrews Parish. Makes notation of "Negros List" naming the enslaved men, women, and children by first name as well as their age. The names of the male slaves are listed as Frank, Dick, "Lummere," Moses, K Joe, Joe, Caesar, Harry, Hector, Charles, Solomon, Senor, Pompy and Skye. The names of the female slaves are listed as Nancy, Cloei, Jeney, Nany, Amelea, Linda, Deana, Judy, Peggy, Perida, "Christan," "Baby," Harriet, Tena, "B. Mulery," "Mury" and Celia.

[![Tax Return of J.H.M and John Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/271783/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/271783)

Date:
1832
Description:
Handwritten poems by James Drayton. One is untitled, the others: "Poetry"; "The Newspaper"; "Beautiful Ballad" (incomplete)

[![Poetry: Untitled; ](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23849/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25700)

Date:
1863-11-06
Description:
Bright is unprepared to met the increased wage Drayton has set for his slave Isaac. He requests Drayton settle for $20 per month.

[![George Bright to James S. Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23833/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25619)

Date:
1864-11-22
Description:
Note to accompany $20 sent for wages of Isaac, Drayton's slave.

[![George Bright to James Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23832/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25615)

Description:
Correspondence from Charles Drayton III., to Mr. Carlisle describing "an slight altercation" between Charles Drayton I., and Banastre Tarleton during the Revolutionary War

[![Charles Drayton III., to Mr. Carlisle](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23824/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25582)

Date:
1863-03-03
Description:
Kanapaux certifies that Isaac Norwood's wife, Mary and her two sons are "entitled to bounty of the Soldiers Relief Association". Calculations for relief on back.

[![Certificate](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23823/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25576)

Date:
1854-02-26
Description:
A lengthy and searching "sketch of my life", by James Drayton. Written in the form a of confessional, Drayton reflects on his childhood, education, the death of his first wife (Louisa Elford), the purpose of his existence and his strong feelings for the recipient.

[![James S. Drayton to ](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23836/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25643)

Description:
A list of clothing and quantities.

[![Drayton List of Clothing](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/271766/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/271766)

Date:
1867-06-30
Description:
Writing to Drayton in Texas, Wienges discusses a storm in Charleston, General Daniel Edgar Sickles, the display of the national flag, and the will of "Uncle Jacob"

[![Conrad Wienges to James Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23828/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25598)

Date:
1856-01-24
Description:
Conveyance of property by Thomas and John Drayton to James Drayton. Inclusive of a lot on Coming Street and twenty-two slaves

[![Conveyance](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23829/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25602)

Description:
This document is a correspondence to Dr. Drayton requesting furniture.

[![Correspondence to Dr. Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/271782/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/271782)

Date:
1863-12-13
Description:
Ramsay denies John Drayton's charges of fraud regarding the slaves' rations. Supported by remarks of Col. J.W. Robertson on back.

[![William Ramsay to D.B. Harris](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23868/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25784)

Description:
A document discussing the discharge of a patient named "Marton Starling." Makes notation regarding two slaves, referenced as "negroes" named Elick and Humphrey.

[![Drayton Family Doctor's Discharge Note](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/271779/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/271779)

Description:
A letter to Dr. John Drayton discussing the death of a slave named "Elick" as well as information regarding the slave "Mort" or "Martin."

[![Letter to Dr. John Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/271777/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/271777)

Date:
1863-08-03
Description:
Gilpillin requests that Drayton identify the cause of death for two slaves under his care. The information is required by the slaveowners.

[![William Gilpillin to John Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23866/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25775)

Date:
1864-10-07
Description:
Laval instructs Drayton to send duplicate drafts requesting the disbursement of the appropriation for Soldiers' Families. The initial drafts were signed by Drayton. The duplicates are instead to be signed by the Parish Chairman. Copy.

[![W.J. Laval to John Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23865/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25771)

Date:
1844-09-11
Description:
Frean cannot communicate with Major J.A. Leland regarding what Drayton is owed; he relates his good wishes regarding Drayton's forthcoming marriage to Louisa Elford.

[![Thomas Frean to James S. Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23862/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25759)

Date:
1844-08-21
Description:
Frean believes Drayton's claim for payment from Major J.A. Leland will be successful

[![Thomas Frean to James S. Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23861/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25754)

Date:
1844-10-23
Description:
Frean informs Drayton that J.A. Leland has dispatched a letter to Drayton the previous day

[![Thomas Frean to James S. Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23863/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25763)

Date:
1844-07-12
Description:
Frean offers his condolences concerning the death of Charles Drayton, James' father; discusses his work as a surveyor and James' prospects for employment

[![Thomas Frean to James S. Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23859/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25744)

Date:
1865-05-02
Description:
Note accompanying a mare sent to Drayton in Columbia.

[![Simon Taylor to John Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23852/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25712)

Date:
1844-07-29
Description:
Frean describes his poor financial situation and is frustrated his cannot contact Major J.A. Leland

[![Thomas Frean to James S. Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23860/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25749)

1844-04-24

Correspondence about Drayton's work as a surveyor and when he might be paid

Trenholm requests that Drayton agree to extend his mining lease at Drayton Hall. Estimates of mining machinery are included.

1869-01-11
Description:
Trenholm requests his lease of Drayton Hall be extended.

A document listing items for medicinal use and associated figures.

A document listing items and associated figures.

A document listing items and associated figures.

Date:
1863-03-12
Description:
Concerning the organisation of the St. Andrews Parish Board for the "relief of the soldiers families".

[![John Drayton to unidentified](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23839/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25655)

Date:
1869-03-15
Description:
Matthews writes concerning the mining lease for Drayton Hall by a Philadelphia Company

[![J. Fraser Matthews to John Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23834/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25625)

Date:
1865-05-19
Description:
John writes to James in Charleston about his desire to come to town to raise money but his unwillingness to take the required Federal oath of allegiance. He inquires if James or someone else who has taken the oath may conduct business on his behalf so that he can avoid the oath. Drayton requests information about the "guards on the \[Charleston\] wharves and river" and notes his intention of leaving the country. He believes it is best for the "\[Federal administration\] to pile on the agony". Also mentions witnessing Union troops chasing Governor Magrath to Columbia.

[![John Drayton to James S. Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23837/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25647)

Date:
1863
Description:
Copy. Drayton complains that military personnel are siphoning the rations of the enslaved laborers. Correspondence is forwarded several times.

[![John Drayton to R.L. Singletary](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23838/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25651)

Date:
1864-04-23
Description:
Lewis offers Drayton employment attending his work party at Dorchester Road

[![John Lewis to John Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23841/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25663)

Date:
1844-10-21
Description:
Leland criticizes Drayton for his inflated bill and instructs him to make several amendments.

[![J.A. Leland to James S. Drayton](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23835/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25630)

1863
Description:
Unidentified correspondent idenitifies irregularities in the slaves' rations.

Date:
1837-1867
Description:
Handwritten poem by member of Drayton Family about a elderly neighbor

[![Poetry: The Fine Old Maiden Lady](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23847/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25690)

Date:
1861-1865
Description:
Handwritten poem by James S. Drayton, urging southern men to rise and fight the Union forces

[![Poetry: Song for the South](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23846/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25686)

Date:
1837-1867
Description:
Multiple prints of J. S. Drayton

Date:
1864-10-30
Description:
Instructions on how to stitch gloves. Incomplete correspondence on back.

[![Stitching instructions](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23856/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25730)

Date:
1865-02-20
Description:
Johnson instructs Pringle to send over "Drayton's train" as he is "only waiting for him" and the trains of two others. Crossed out notes on back.

[![John Johnson to Major Pringle](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23840/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25659)

Date:
1862-03
Description:
Drayton makes special requisitions for medicines for the slaves he attends at Bee's Ferry and at Camp Hinson

[![Lists of medical supplies](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23842/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25667)

Date:
1840-1867
Description:
A literary manuscript by James Drayton

[![Literary Manuscript](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23843/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25674)

Date:
1837-1867
Description:
Handwritten poem by James Drayton about a conversation held in a prison between a father and his daughter during the reign of the English King Richard III

[![Poetry: Untitled](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23848/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25694)

Date:
1861
Description:
Tax receipt of Thomas Drayton, Brazoria County, Texas. Includes tax on land, slaves, horses and miscellaneous property

[![Tax receipt](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23857/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25734)

Date:
1837-1869
Description:
Plan of building facade and roof. Structure very similar to (if not), Drayton Hall mansion. Miscellaneous calculations included.

[![Sketches](https://rspace.library.cofc.edu/iiif/image/23853/full/200,/0/default.jpg)](https://lcdl.library.cofc.edu/lcdl/catalog/lcdl:25716)
